data.title: Mid to low cap energy stocks with the weakest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off this week, we look at mid-to-low market cap energy stocks with weak earnings momentum.

These stocks face the most challenging analyst outlooks, spanning various subsectors including oil and gas exploration and production, equipment and services, storage and transportation, and refining and marketing.

Each stock on this list has received an ‘F’ grade for EPS revisions, the lowest possible rating. This grade indicates that analysts have made significant downward revisions to their earnings estimates for these companies.

Here are the mid-low cap energy stocks with the worst EPS revision grades.

  1. Advantage Energy (AAVVF) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  2. Forum Energy Technologies (FET) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  3. Genesis Energy, L.P.  (GEL) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  4. Golar LNG (GLNG) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  5. GeoPark (GPRK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  6. HighPeak Energy (HPK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  7. Kodiak Gas Services (KGS) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  8. Kinetik Holdings (KNTK) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  9. Oil States International (OIS) — EPS Revision Grade: F

  10. OPAL Fuels (OPAL) — EPS Revision Grade: F

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Merck signals appetite for multi-billion-dollar acquisitions
data.description:
• Pharmaceutical giant Merck & Co (MRK) said it is looking to strike more deals, pointing to opportunities for acquisitions worth “multi tens of billions” of dollars.
  • “We are not limited from a balance sheet — it’s more where do we see strategic opportunity,” Chief Executive Officer Rob Davis said at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference in San Francisco.
  • Davis said the company’s progress to date has given it visibility into more than $70 billion in commercial opportunities through the mid-2030s.
  • The WSJ reported on Friday that Merck was in discussions to acquire the cancer-drug biotech for about $30 billion. Revolution Medicines (RVMD) however, declined to comment on recent M&A speculation.

data.title: Core CPI inflation comes in slightly cooler than expected in December
data.description:
December Consumer Price Index: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Tuesday.

+2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy): +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior.

+2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Beretta Holding threatens proxy fight at Sturm Ruger - FT
data.description:
Beretta Holding is threatening a proxy contest at rival gunmaker Sturm Ruger (RGR) after becoming its largest shareholder. Sturm Ruger rose 1% in premarket trading.

Beretta is working with advisers to prepare for a proxy contest, including searching for possible candidates to nominate to Ruger’s board ahead of an annual meeting later this year, according to a Financial Times report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

In October,  Ruger  adopted a so-called poison pill to block a hostile takeover after Beretta disclosed a 9% stake in its rival. Beretta has since upped its stake to almost 10%, according to the report.

Ruger didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from the FT. Beretta declined to comment.

data.title: Scorpio Tankers provides updates on liquidity, debt, vessel purchase commitments
data.description:
• Scorpio Tankers (STNG) on Tuesday said that it held $783.9 million in availability under its revolving credit facilities as of January 9, 2026.
  • Since October 28, 2025, the company sold its remaining 3,551,794 common shares in DHT Holdings at an average price of $13.40 per share.
  • In November 2025, the company closed on the previously announced sales of the 2020 built MR product tanker, STI Maestro for $42.0 million and the 2014 built MR product tankers, STI Battery, STI Venere, and STI Milwaukee, each for $32.0 million.
  • In early December 2025, the company closed on the previously announced sales of the 2014 built MR product tanker STI Yorkville for $32.0 million and the 2019 built LR2 product tanker STI Lobelia for $61.2 million.
  • The table below summarizes the company’s previously announced commitments to purchase newbuilding vessels consisting of two Very Large Crude Carriers with deliveries expected in the second half of 2028, two LR2s with deliveries expected in the third quarter of 2027, and four MRs with a delivery expected in each of the second and fourth quarter of 2026 and two deliveries in 2027.
  In millions of USD Amount  
  Q1 2026 - paid $ 14.2  
  Q1 2026 - to be paid   26.7  
  Q2 2026   57.6  
  Q3 2026   14.2  
  Q4 2026   59.2  
  2027   212.6  
  2028   188.3  
    $ 572.8  
         

 

  • STNG +1.07% premarket to $55.52.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Red Cat sees Q4 revenue between $24M and $26.5M
data.description:
• Red Cat Holdings (RCAT)  on Tuesday said fourth quarter revenue is expected to be between $24 million and $26.5 million, representing an approximate 1,842% increase compared to the $1.3 million reported in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Full year 2025 revenue is expected to be between $38 million and $41 million, representing an approximate 153% increase compared to the $15.6 million reported for the full year 2024.
  • RCAT +5.52% premarket to $12.6.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 revenues are $35.41M and $20.92M, respectively.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trinity Capital reports $2.1 billion of new commitments, $1.5 billion funded in 2025
data.description:
• Trinity Capital (TRIN) on Tuesday it originated a record $2.1 billion of new investment commitments in 2025 and funded a record $1.5 billion of investments, according to a fourth-quarter and full-year portfolio update.
  • The alternative asset manager said it funded $435 million of investments in the fourth quarter, including $305 million in secured loans, $106 million in equipment financings and $24 million in warrant and equity investments.
  • New commitments in the quarter totaled $543 million, which was comprised of $329 million in secured loans, $190 million in equipment financings and $24 million in equity investments.
  • For the full year, Trinity said funded investments comprised $1.1 billion in secured loans, $303 million in equipment financings and $59 million in warrant and equity investments.

  • The company originated $1.6 billion in secured loan commitments, $429 million in equipment financings and $60 million in equity investments during the year.

  • Trinity said it received $826 million in proceeds from repayments and exits in 2025.
  • The company is scheduled to report full financial results on February 25, 2026.
  • TRIN +0.18% premarket to $15.98.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SunPower expands equity line of credit to $55M
data.description:
• SunPower (SPWR) entered into an amendment to increase its equity line of credit with White Lion Capital from $30M to $55M.
  • The expanded ELOC provides SunPower with additional flexible access to capital at its discretion.
  • Under the ELOC structure, White Lion Capital agrees to purchase newly issued shares.
  • The facility allows SunPower to manage liquidity needs while minimizing potential shareholder dilution compared to a traditional equity offering.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp to acquire BTIG for up to $1B
data.description:
U.S. Bancorp (USB) on Tuesday announced a definitive agreement to acquire the financial services firm BTIG for up to $1B.

The purchase price is targeted to be $725M, comprising $362.5M of cash and 6,600,594 shares of common stock. The deal also includes up to an additional $275M of cash consideration payable over three years.

USB shares were -0.15% pre-market to $54.31.

The transaction is expected to have negligible 2026 EPS impact and decrease U.S. Bancorp's common equity Tier 1 capital ratio by ~12 basis points.

The two companies have worked closely over the past decade.

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter.

BTIG leadership team will continue to lead the business after joining U.S. Bancorp. BTIG CEO LeRoy will report to Stephen Philipson, vice chair and head of wealth, corporate, commercial, and institutional banking at U.S. Bancorp.
